original: You’re standing before Hana, whom is working the bar. A huskar like most of the locals, she’s surprisingly tall and willowy in stature despite her older, maternal appearance, making her curvy waist line and immodest bosom subtle in her silhouette. Beyond her mere figure the woman is raven haired, the black strands framing her head in a bob cut with a bang hanging across the side of her face, concealing the black, simplistic eyepatch over her right eye in the process. The usual ausar fur lines her arms and, you presume, her legs - though you can’t see the latter through the bar.

\n\nUnlike typical ausar, though, she also has fur puffing up around her neckline as if she is wearing a particularly fluffy scarf or shawl, given her huskar nature. All of the fur is black, but you can see a few aging gray strands stand out both in her hair and fuzzier fur. To accentuate this, or simply to avoid being too warm, the beige, cozy and body hugging wool sweater she wears opens in the chest, though where there’d be cleavage on any other woman lies a massive puff of fur overshadowing it
, disappointingly

\n\nHana is certainly the older sort, giving off a maternal sort of vibe from her appearance. However, her terse acting, unsmiling demeanor goes against the body image. As you’re eyeballing her she’s quietly watching you in the corner of her single eye as she otherwise works, cleaning mugs out with a thick looking cloth that hums with some technological purpose. While the barkeep is certainly waiting to serve you, it doesn’t look like she’s about to say something first.

“Well, I wouldn’t mind a drink.” You quip, leaning against the wood stained bartop expectantly.

\n\nHana merely nods her head and lifts a hand away from her rag to tap at some interface hidden away beneath the bar, silently. Your codex chirps soon after with the menu - after a brief permission prompt, anyway. It’s surprisingly robust, so much so that you spend a good minute or so just flicking through the multitude of menus. Some of it also gets prohibitively expensive, too, but you’ve come to expect that with some esoteric vintages. Quietly you narrow down the listings in your head, ready to make an easy order.

There are a dizzying amount of options just for mead alone, but you end up just going with one of the simple, cheap varieties. Can’t get too special with mead - but you can’t go wrong, either. As you tap the screen to make your order, you jealously note that there’s quite a discount for local residents - it might as well be free!

\n\nBy the time you look up from your codex again the “busy” barkeep has already placed the pale-golden drink in front of you in a tall glass, complete with a little glass coaster. No sooner are you served, she’s back busily cleaning glasses and paying you little mind again, leaving you with nothing to do but relax and enjoy the smooth, easy going, honey sweet booze. There really is nothing wrong with going for mead.

Flicking through the choices, you come across something particularly eye catching; one of the local flavors, ostentatiously some sort of tourist drink. You’re a tourist here, so why not? Tapping your codex, you immediately hear a dismissive huff from Hana across the bar as she gets to work on it. Must get a lot of tourists, then.

\n\n“It’s nothing special. People hear about all the savicite around and how it has changed some of the creatures living out here, so they want to get in on it. Of course, not many people can actually survive the trip outside.” Hana begins demurely, her tone sounding wooden and rehearsed. “So it’s the ‘psi’ retreatment; it’ll mess with your head for a bit and then you’re perfectly fine. In reality it’s just a mild sedative mixed into the drink.”

\n\nDrink might be a bit of an exaggeration; when she sets the glass down it’s little more than a shot, the novelty coaster it rests on feeling entirely unnecessary. Ah well, you’ve already bought it. Down the hatch!

You feel fuzzy for a moment. A kinda weird itch in the back of your head, like you’re forgetting something. The feeling passes, though, and you’re left just sitting there.
\n\nWasn’t something supposed to happen? You don’t really feel any different.
\n\nLooking up to Hana with a quizzical expression you’re answered with a quiet, indifferent shrug.
\n\nUgh, what a rip off.
For a brief moment you become woozy, your [pc.skin] feeling flush, particularly on your arms.

\n\nYour vision grows hazy, the warm lights of the bar and central fireplace superimposing over one another into a blurry, blinding mess. Shapes swirl in your eyes - no, in your head? You can barely parse it and soon you don’t have to, the swirling mess shifting away into a white, empty void.
\n\nDid you pass out?

\n\nNo, you can still feel the bar beside you and hear the banter of the bar patrons, though both seem somewhat distant. The odd aches of your body gradually disappear until everything is just... muted. It’s there, sure, but you can barely recognize any of the stimuli your senses register.
\n\nYour mind feels blissfully clear and empty. There’s no concern about the race against your cousin, about the consequences of your adventures, even drive to continue on.
\n\nNot right now.
\n\nIt’s just...
\n\nNothing. It’s nice. It’s also rather terrifying, though you don’t come to that conclusion until the effect starts to wear off.
\n\nA mixed feeling of both euphoria and nausea starts to overwhelm you, properly pulling you out of that drug assisted reprieve.
\n\nYou swallow the urge to retch, at least, and the world comes back into focus. Whoa.

Frisky Dingo, huh? You’ve seen some of the ads around - it’s some mainstream brand coming from the ausar homeworlds. It’s popular, so why not?

\n\nTapping it in earns you a faint scoff from Hana just before she spins around on her heels, reaching at something in one of the lower cabinets. It gives you a brief glimpse at her skirt-clad rear. The
pleasing
view lasts mere seconds before Hana hoists herself back upright, planting the chilled, bottle-sweat glistening order down in front of you with a prominent clink of glass-on-glass.

\n\nThe colorful caricature of a dog greets you on the labeling. Hana is gracious enough to pop off the archaic tin cap with a bottle opener for you before getting back to her barkeeping tasks, leaving you with your drink.
\n\nA swig later and the verdict is in. Shitty, painfully mediocre commercial beer. Not that you really expected anything else.

One of the menu options is simply a picture of an empty mug and the listing of ‘the party game,’ with no additional information to speak of. Curious, you give it a tap and look up to Hana, expectantly, taking note of her brief surprise as she gets to work on the order.

\n\n“Oh, you don’t know? Heh... it’s a local game, each day it’s a different mug filled with four or five different drinks on the menu. If you guess them all correctly and finish your drink you get a little prize. Just some money and fanfare, really. It’s a challenge made for alcoholics.” The woman finishes with a wry smirk, planting a whole, frothing stein down in front of you. It’s... rather alarming. Even merely sitting it down rings out a challenging clunk of glass onto the coaster. The color of the drink is suspect, an off-amber from mixed pale and dark drinks. This might have been a mistake.

\n\nTentatively, you take a sip. Muted, mixed flavors all challenge one another for dominance across your taste buds, causing you to wince - and Hana to laugh. Yeah, this is definitely a mistake. It’s made for locals, people who have actually slurped their way through the menu over time. You realize about half way through the stein that some of these flavors are actually pretty strong and it’s hitting you hard. Some form of intrepid spirit keeps you sipping it down, though, and eventually you’re greeted with the bemused expression of Hana, albeit hazy through the bottom of the glass.

\n\n“I’m not going to bother quizzing you on what was specifically in there, but can you guess how many drinks there were?” She is looking pretty haughty, though you can’t really say how much what with your head screaming at you already.
\n\n... Maybe eight?
\n\n“Just four, heh. Here, have some water. Pace yourself in the future, tourists stumbling around on the ice outside is bad for publicity.”

“You want to know about the colony?” Hana sounds a bit surprised, arching a brow over her one good eye. When you asked the first time she merely brushed you off, but it only took a bit of pressuring - you are genuinely interested, not just making small talk. Your honest enthusiasm earns you a response in kind from the barkeep, who purses her lips as she collects her thoughts.

\n\n“Honestly, there isn’t too much to say. That’s... not quite true, clearly, but Irestead’s story is the same of a lot of backwater colonies, especially ones just hanging around for research and mining. It’s just cold. This place could be called the ‘capital’ of all of Uveto, but functionally that’s more the station. Still, it’s the heart of the colonial network. But... you could get any of that from a news article, couldn’t you?” The barkeep pauses, looking thoughtful again. Doesn’t look like she plays tourist guide often.

\n\n“Before Rhenworld got involved this was all just a lackluster research station, really. Once someone found out there was money under the ice, well. Things changed. Suddenly there’s a reason to make sure we’re all cozy, happy and wanting to invite friends. Living conditions got better, the streets got heaters so it wasn’t just ice slabs all the time. It got... well, comfortable isn’t really right, but certainly hospitable. You can actually go outside. Heh, most colonies are just a big, parked freighter loaded with crap and a prayer that it can take off ten years later.”

\n\n“Despite that, it’s... listen, I’m pretty cushy with this bar. I could sell it - or get someone else to run it, anyway - and live in a resort in the core worlds if I really wanted to. Most of the people here are just manual laborers, roped into a job out of some trade school or other. Laborers working beside the machines - or technicians, if they actually came from higher education - trying to make a decent pay cheque. It could be better, sure, this is a rush after all. But it could be a lot worse, too.”

\n\n“Although I could really do without the Stormguards.” Hana grunts, finally finishing and, unceremoniously, going back to her mug cleaning. It doesn’t look like she intends to embellish anymore, particularly about anyone specific.

Hana is a pretty big woman nestled deep in a bar of local regulars who would all gladly come to the aid of a friend, no matter the situation. It’s a dangerous prospect, but your curiosity knows no limits; you measure up some volume of tact and, gently, inquire about her eyepatch.

\n\nThe immediate response puts you on edge; Hana tenses up, the hand wrist-deep in the mug freezing up. Her one eye narrows, the hazel hue becoming a dagger-like squint. It might be your imagination, but you swear some of the rowdy banter between the patrons quiets down, too. But that had to be coincidence, no way they could hear you, could they?

\n\n“My eyepatch.” Hana repeated, the tension growing as the woman gently places the mug down on the counter top with a heavy thunk. Maybe this was a bad idea, though with the barkeep leaning forward, both hands firmly on the bar, it is far too late for second thoughts.

\n\n“This... this was my reward for somebody else’s mistake. From someone playing hero - and failing. It came when my dear husband got himself killed trying to save someone else.... I don’t want to talk about it, traveler.” Her tone is gravelly, stern with the unmistakable harsh edge of somebody speaking through gritted teeth. A sore subject, obviously. “I am
incorrigible
. I can’t ‘fix’ it. I don’t want to fix it. Don’t ask about it again.”

\n\nYou did your part as an attentive and loving mate – which amounted to little more than emotional and sexual support, trapped as you were on the island that housed her lair. Still, by the time the fourth month rolled around, you were there for every little step of [frostwyrm.name] ‘laying,’ as she constantly referred it.

\n\nThe egg was no larger than an average, healthy [pc.originalRace] newborn baby. Its shell was smooth and sturdy all around; you doubt you’d be able to crack it open even if you wanted to. When all was said and done, you snuggle up against [frostwyrm.name] once more, sharing the space with her freshly-laid egg.

\n\nYou ask [frostwyrm.name] if you two should think of a name for the kip"+(1===t?"":"s")+", but she voices against it; as she told you before, her kind’s traditions don’t include names, only titles. That said, you’ll know to tell each kip apart through your psionic link with each of them.
\n\nWhile the most tedious part is over for you both, you don’t have to leave just yet if you don’t want.
\n\nUnfortunately, all that time waiting produced no eggs...
4 Months...
4 Months...
Leave

You nestle yourself against [frostwyrm.name]’s body, getting warm and comfy against her big body. You tell her that you have nothing major that needs attention within the next year. All you need to do is send a message every now and again to let everyone know you’re not dead, and everything should be smooth.
Your daughter coos delightedly; you feel her large arms and deft fingers play and tickle at your body, letting you know how much she appreciates your decision.
Your daughters coo delightedly; you feel their large arms and deft fingers play and tickle at your body, letting you know how much they appreciate your decision.

\n\nI will not feign that I know much about your kind and your culture, [pc.name], she says. She has a noticeable upward lilt in her voice – she’s overjoyed at your decision. But you must not feign that this was not a difficult decision for you. I am grateful you have chosen to be with your mate.
You get yourself comfortable: it’s going to be a while yet...
4 Months...
\n\n...Although, you can use the power of CHEATS to warp time and reality to complete the year-long wait without much passing time.
Cheat Wait
TIME CHEAT IS ON\n\n

For the most part, the next four months were fairly mundane and peaceful, if simple: you, [frostwyrm.name], and Nykke all kept each other company within her lair while you patiently waited for your kip"+(1===t?"":"s")+" to hatch.

While you’re glad to finally be done with the extra weight, in some primordial part of your brain, you not only miss the feeling of the pregnancy but you also want to get right back to it. You have a lot of propagating to do for the Frostwyrms!

\n\nBy month five, you notice something rather unusual about the egg"+(1===t?": it seems":"s: they seem")+" to be growing. [frostwyrm.name] is aware that that isn’t normal for the rest of the universe, but Nykke needs reminding: every other egg in the universe is hatched when the creature inside it grows too big for it. You’ve never heard of an egg growing with the creature inside it. However, [frostwyrm.name] had assured you that this is perfectly normal for Frostwyrm eggs, and you’d trust her judgment over your own, so, that’s that.

\n\nThe sounds you heard from the kip remained, even when the egg was no longer inside you; whenever you asked to hear them, they seemed to get louder and, in a way, articulate: sounds would repeat and the melody would be punctuated by a dip in pitch, similar to how sentences are formed.
\n\nHalfway through month six

, you began to understand very basic ‘words’ from the growing kip"+(1===t?"":"s")+"; one sound would mean ‘Qim,’ and another would mean ‘warm,’ and you’d know the kip would be asking for [frostwyrm.name] to warm it up.

Once the kip"+(s&&r?"s":"")+" that grew within your mate"+(s&&r?"s made their":" made its")+" psionic connection with you, you were finally included in the conversations between the "+(s&&r?"three":"two")+" siblings.

, you begin to hear odd sounds at all times bellow themselves into your mind; [frostwyrm.name] tells you that "+(s&&r?"their your kips, and that they have finally made a link to their Qal, establishing you as their":"it’s your kip, and that it’s finally made a link to its Qal, establishing you as its")+" parent and attempting to communicate with you. Nykke, instinctively understanding the sounds, is beside herself with giddiness.

\n\nBy the end of month seven, the kip"+(1===t?" within was":"s within were")+" capable of making proper sentences, and by the start of month eight, you were having short conversations together. The"+(1===t?" egg was":" eggs were")+" growing at a rather alarming rate:

towards the end of month eight, "+(1===t?"it was":"they were")+" about as large as you – and, taking into account the kip"+(1===t?" within, it":"s within them, they")+" likely weighed significantly more than you do now
towards the end of month eight, "+(1===t?"it was":"they were")+" already substantially larger and heavier than you
. Thank goodness you didn’t try to give birth to one of these!

\n\nOne fateful day at the end of month eight, you hear a sharp crackling noise within the lair; you’re startled at first, afraid "+(1===t?"the egg":"one of the eggs")+" had tipped over, but you’re relieved when you realize: it’s finally time! Your kip"+(1===t?" pushes its way through the tough interior of the shell, using its":"s push their way through the tough interior of the shells, using their")+" powerful wings to puncture it and crack it apart. You, [frostwyrm.name], and Nykke all watch rapturously as your offspring take"+(1===t?"s its first breath and the solid shell cracks to splinters around it.":" their first breath and the solid shells crack to splinters around them.")

\n\nYou finally get an answer to the question you had been wondering ever since its conception: what would it look like? What you see is a seven-foot tall, bipedal dragon; it has icy-white scales all along its body, with layered, sky-blue, armored scales along its front, from neck to crotch. Like Nykke, it has a humanoid face, with two piercing red eyes. It has massive wings, their bones reaching higher than its head and all the way down to its ankles, and with a wingspan as wide as they are tall. It has two legs, yet four arms; and, as of yet, it lacks any sort of sexual characteristic. Surely those will develop later.

\n\nYou finally get an answer to the question you had been wondering ever since their conception: what would they look like? What you see are seven-foot tall, bipedal dragons; they have icy-white scales all along their bodies, with layered, sky-blue, armored scales along their front, from neck to crotch. Like Nykke, they have humanoid faces, with two piercing red eyes. They have massive wings, their bones reaching higher than their heads and all the way down to their ankles, and with a wingspan as wide as they are tall. They each have two legs, yet four arms; and, as of yet, they lack any sort of sexual characteristic. Surely those will develop later.

\n\nYou look to Nykke for her reaction, seeing that your newborn kip"+(1===t?" bears":"s bear")+" a powerful, nearly-uncanny resemblance to her... but with pure-white scales, unlike her own pitch-black. But, from the wide-eyes and the goofy grin on her lips, the look of your kip’s scales is one of the last things on her mind. She has a new addition to her family, and, right now, that’s what’s important to her, above all else.

\n\nYour kip is exhausted; it took all its strength to break free, and the first thing it wants to do is rest. It doesn’t know how to walk, and when it tries, it immediately falls onto its face. You, [frostwyrm.name], and Nykke are on it quickly; looks like any other plans you had for the day have been changed.

\n\n[frostwyrm.name] wraps you, Nykke, and your kip into a huddle, sharing her body warmth and allowing it to rest. It clutches and squeezes at various body parts between you three; when it goes for you, [frostwyrm.name] introduces you as it’s Qal; when it goes for either [frostwyrm.name] or Nykke, they’re both introduced as it’s Qim. You know it’s unusual for a kip to have two Qims, and you can’t imagine it’s particularly instinctual for your two mates to both introduce themselves as a single kip’s Qim, but, to your delight, neither of them have any trouble willingly taking and sharing the title. Becoming a single family unit is more natural to them than being conflicted over a title.

\n\n[frostwyrm.name] wraps you and your kip into a huddle, sharing her body warmth and allowing it to rest. It clutches and squeezes at various body parts between you both; when it goes for [frostwyrm.name], she introduces herself as its Qim. When it reaches for you, [frostwyrm.name] introduces you as their Qal.

\n\nWith the introductions out of the way, you broach the obvious topic hanging in the air between you two: your firstborn kip has black scales, whereas [frostwyrm.name]’s are snow-white. You ask her if this is a common thing, and that its scales will bleach over time.

\n\n“No,” she answers. You feel a number of conflicting emotions seep through the psionic link between you two – parental love; unconditional affection; and total relief... but, also, hesitation. “I had told you before that our resultant kip would inherit the strongest traits of us both, but... I had never been privy to an abnormality such as this.”
\n\nShould you two be worried?

\n\n“Without a form to disappear into the Wet Dust, our kip may have difficulty hunting when it matures. It is without an important element of the hunt to aid it.” While her words are sobering, her parental instincts and emotions do not stop their flood over the link between you. “Our kip is born healthy, my Qal, and, for the moment, that is all I care to know.”
\n\nThe most exciting part of rearing your Frostwyrm hybrid children is over with; just another four months until "+(1===t?"its":"they’re")+" fully mature.
4 Months...
TIME CHEAT IS ON\n\n
The final four months of your child-rearing are definitely the most interesting and educational.

Your kips have gargantuan and imposing forms, even though they’re so young; they appear as though they should be capable of walking and lifting and hunting right away, but their muscles, while strong enough to carry themselves, are good for only that. They require proper nursing before they’re strong enough and smart enough to fend for themselves.

\n\nLuckily, that doesn’t last long. The ninth month is you, your two mates, and your kips bonding with each other and familiarizing yourselves with each other’s forms and titles. They quickly learn that you’re their Qal and to address you as one; it takes them a bit longer to learn to address both [frostwyrm.name] and Nykke as their Qim, rather than one or the other, but they figure it out. You and Nykke, sharing a similar form as your kips, teach them to walk, while [frostwyrm.name] teaches them to fly. You teach them language, and their biology allows them to speak verbally and not purely through psionics, while [frostwyrm.name] teaches them how to hunt and how to use their psionic strength similarly to herself. And while Nykke might not have been the most accomplished hunter, it was only because her scales made her impossible to camouflage – she had mastered multiple techniques in how to move, sneak, and kill her prey in order to survive, and she was all too happy to pass on what she knew to her clutch, fulfilling her duty as a real, earnest Qim to a family.

\n\nThrough month ten and eleven, your kips spend most of their time with their two Qims; [frostwyrm.name] and Nykke spend much of their time communicating with the clutch’s newest family members, enlightening them to the strengths and utilities of the psionic powers unique to their Frostwyrm lineage. Through [frostwyrm.name], your kips are taught how to act; how to speak; and how to address their parents; and through Nykke, they’re taught certain truths of the world, and how best to survive in such an unforgiving climate, especially with a biology like hers – not being a pureblood Frostwyrm has its disadvantages, but, thankfully, your newest kips have pure-white scales, and they’ll never know the difficulties Nykke had to put up with her whole life.
\n\nWith their emotional growth comes a real sense of maturity, and with that, a sense of identity: taking after their Qims, they almost instinctively chooses to be referred to by female pronouns.

\n\nYour girls are naturally inquisitive, asking you why you and Nykke look so different from [frostwyrm.name] and what the differences between you three are. They ask you how you met them both, which, naturally, was followed-up by asking how you came to be the Qal to two Qims, and where they, as your kips, belongs in this world.

It requires proper nursing before it’s strong enough and smart enough to fend for itself.

\n\nYou answer each of her questions dutifully and truthfully, explaining the differences in biology between you, Nykke, and [frostwyrm.name] in the clearest, simplest ways you can. Questions that are answered are beget with more questions, which you encourage.

You hope this doesn’t make you speciesist, or a bad parent, but you sometimes have difficulty telling your daughters apart, and they all look similar enough to match the same description.

\n\nYour daughter is extremely tall, standing at about ten feet tall from sole to scalp, not counting her wings. Similar to her Qim, she has six limbs, although they’re shaped quite differently: your daughter walks on two plantigrade feet, and she has four arms, two each sprouting from her left and right shoulders. She has five toes on each foot and five fingers on each hand. Each digit is capped with a razor sharp talon, no less lethal than her Qim’s, although they’re considerably shorter.

\n\nEvery one of your kips has covered in white scales that glisten in the reflective light of the icy lair – every one of them, except for your firstborn, which stands apart from the rest by having a body covered in onyx-black scales. Along her front, going from her lower jaw to the inside of her thighs, she has softer, yielding, malleable gray scales, although you doubt many conventional weapons would be able to wound her. You ask "+(void 0!==flags.NYKKE_MET&&e?"Nykke":"her")+" if she’s ever really wondered why her Qim and her Qal look so differently. [frostwyrm.name] is very large, and you’re... well, you’re not. [frostwyrm.name] has six limbs, and you only have four. There’s a lot of differences.

\n\n“I never really even thought about it,” she answers happily. “You are my Qal, and [frostwyrm.name] is my Qim. Qims looking like her and Qals looking like you was just... a truth of the world that I accepted
. Ask any one of us; we’d all tell you the same thing
. I don’t even see you as different.”

\n\nYou reply that Qims and Qals can come in any shape or size, but the truth is that you and [frostwyrm.name] aren’t of the same species, and that there are millions of different species in the world. Rather than reply, you feel your kip use her psionics to probe your mind for the definitions of some words. “A million is a lot!” she exclaims. You correct her – there are millions. More than you could rightly count.
\n\n“So what are the differences between you and the Qim?” she asks, inspired by your line of questioning. “Or between you and me?”
\n\nYou start with the size, although that’s an obvious one
. [frostwyrm.name] has wings, which help her get around the planet at a much faster pace, and you don’t have something like that
. When [frostwyrm.name] walks around, she does so using her six legs, but you and her walk around on two. You only have two arms, while [frostwyrm.name] doesn’t have any, and your kip has four.

\n\n“Why is that?” she asks, and you answer that you don’t know, but your best guess is because [frostwyrm.name] has six limbs, so some wires got crossed when your kip was born and gave her six limbs as well. “What does that mean, that ‘some wires got crossed?’” You... answer that gene-passing is a complicated subject that you’re no expert on, and she’ll figure it out when she has her own mate.

\n\n“Would it... be able to explain why my body is darker than our Qim’s?” she asks, hopeful for an answer to a question that’s clearly been bugging her for some time. You answer that it likely would, but, again, you can only speculate; you don’t have a lot of answers on the subject.
\n\n“So what other differences are there?” she asks. One of her hands reaches for your [pc.crotch], fondling casually, almost innocently in her pursuit of knowledge, at your junk. “
You have a penis and a vagina, just like the Qim and I do, so that’s not any different
You have a penis, just like the Qim, but you don’t have a vagina
You have a vagina, just like the Qim, but you don’t have a penis
You have these meaty things on your chest, just like I do
, although they’re smaller than mine
, and they’re bigger than mine
, but the Qim doesn’t have any.
Her face is a lot longer than ours, too, and she doesn’t have these puffy things around our mouths.”
\n\nYou grit your teeth at "+(void 0===flags.NYKKE_MET?"your kip":"Nykke")+" reminding you that your boobs are smaller than hers, and tell yourself that she meant nothing by it.
\n\n“So what other differences are there?” she asks. She caresses her face with one of her hands. “Her face is a lot longer than ours, too, and she doesn’t have these puffy things around our mouths.”

\n\nYou respond to most of her questions at once by saying that everyone is born a little different: some people have more than others; some are born taller, or with bigger assets. But you remind her that not always is bigger, better, and that she should judge others by their personality and their merits rather than their physical appearance.

\n\n“And these?” she asks, groping and squeezing at her tits. You tell her that those are called ‘breasts,’ and that their primary function is to nurse young with – but they have lots of other feel-good uses, too.
\n\n“How do you ‘nurse’ young with these? That doesn’t make any sense to me.”

\n\nYou reply that she’s asking at a lucky time: you gently grip your boob just beneath the nipple and give it a squeeze. Your [pc.milk] sprays from your [pc.nipple] in thin streams, going every which way.

\n\n"+(void 0===flags.NYKKE_MET?"Your kip":"Nykke")+" gasps in surprise at the sight, then a giddy grin crosses her face. This liquid, called ‘milk,’ is what the young of your kind sustain on until they’re older for other, solid foods.
\n\n“May I have some?” she asks. You reply that she’s... a little above being breastfed at her age, and you lower your [pc.nipple] away from her. She pouts, but respects your decision.

\n\n“What about these?” she asks, drawing a finger across her lips, continuing your biology lesson. You tell her that those are her lips: without those, she wouldn’t be able to talk, or open her mouth to eat, or kiss.
“What is a ‘kiss?’” she asks, and you demonstrate, leaning forward and putting your [pc.lipsChaste] to hers.
\n\n“That felt really good!” she exclaims. You tell her that that is how others of your kind show affection with others. “Can you do that again?”

\n\nYou do so, leaning in to give her another quick peck on her lips. She sighs in delight when your [pc.lipsChaste] meet hers once more. “Can you... can we do that again?” she asks, but you stay firm and tell her that that’s enough for now. She pouts, but respects your decision as her Qal.

You ask "+(void 0!==flags.NYKKE_MET&&e?"Nykke":"your kip")+" if she knows what the word ‘technology’ means. In response, you feel a gentle probing in your mind. “I do now,” she answers, a wry smile on her face. You tell her that that’s cheating – and then she probes your mind once more, probably searching for the word ‘cheat.’ “Sorry. I didn’t know what it meant, and you asked me, so....”
\n\nThat’s fine, you reply, but you broaden your question. Now that she knows what it means, has she ever had any interaction with it? “Nope! I hardly ever leave the lair without you or the Qim.”
\n\nYou ask her to bring you your effects, off in the corner of the lair
. She immediately delegates the task to one of her younger sisters so that she can stay cuddled up with her Qal – typical older-sibling attitude
. Once they’re with you, you rifle through your things, searching for your Codex, and when it’s in hand, you ask your kip to put everything back where it was.

\n\nIn your hand is a small, square device with a glassy screen and a steel back. On its side is a number of buttons: you press one, and the screen hums to life, displaying your last recorded entry. You explain that, in your hand, you hold a device capable of connecting to an invisible network that spans the world many times over; this network contains the collected knowledge of your species’ entire history. You can also use it to communicate with others connected to the same network.

\n\n“I don’t believe you,” she says. “How can this little thing have so much knowledge in it?” You demonstrate: you bring up the codex entry for Frostwyrms. "+(void 0!==flags.NYKKE_MET&&e?"Nykke":"Your kip")+" can’t read its information (you promise yourself that you’ll teach her sometime), but she believes your every word as you read out the entry. You bring up the entry for Uveto, then the entry for other planets and locations you’ve been to.
\n\nYou close your demonstration by contacting your ship: after a moment,

your call is automatically answered by your ship, and you’re given a view of the cockpit, but there’s nobody there to answer. You explain that this is your ship, and you use it to travel vast distances at high speeds – almost like a set of wings. "+(void 0!==flags.NYKKE_MET&&e?"Nykke":"Your kip")+" scrunches her nose as she studies the Codex, and she looks around the metal box, trying to see more into the ship. She presses her nose against the glass, trying to go through it to get into the ship, but she’s stopped by the glass screen.

\n\nThis is just a minor example of the technology your species is capable of – so minor, in fact, that this is considered fairly mundane and typical of its usual standards. Advancements include traveling at speeds many times faster than any Frostwyrm is capable of; medicine capable of curing any disease; transformatives that can change the physical makeup of any person to turn them into whatever they want; there are almost no limits anymore.

\n\nYou flip through various channels on your Codex, explaining the different things you find. When you find clothing, you explain their purpose and appeal; when you find weapons, you explain their necessities and their dangers. When you find television programs, you explain they’re use as information relays and entertainment–

\n\n“Go back to that last one,” she asks you, and you do so. It’s an episode of Steph Irson’s Galactic Huntress. From the looks of it, it’s getting close to the end of the episode, judging from how she’s gotten herself in another gangbang with some planet’s indigenous species. It looks like the Zil from Mhen’ga this time.

\n\n"+(void 0!==flags.NYKKE_MET&&e?"Nykke":"Your kip")+" watches, enraptured. She’s totally taken in by the programming going on in front of her. “What’s going on here?” she asks you innocently, and you explain who Steph is and what her goal is – or, rather, what it’s supposed to be, but more often than not, it always devolves into... exactly what’s happening right now. Her audience tends to prefer it this way.

\n\n"+(void 0!==flags.NYKKE_MET&&e?"Nykke":"Your kip")+" probes your mind once again. “You mean there are individuals that enjoy watching others mate for their entertainment?” You answer that yes, that’s about the long and short of it. “Wow.”
\n\nShe sits in almost stunned silence for a moment as she watches Steph get absolutely railed by two male, and one female, Zil.
“I wonder if-” she begins, but then stops herself, her eyes flitting towards yours for a split second. “Never mind, it’s not important.”
\n\nWhen you switch the programming to something else, she pouts, but ultimately respects your decision as her Qal.
Next

You ask "+(void 0!==flags.NYKKE_MET&&e?"Nykke":"your kip")+" what she thinks of the standards and traditions that are upheld here in [frostwyrm.name]’s lair. “I don’t think I understand the question,” she says, so you elaborate, one step at a time: what does she think about mating with you and [frostwyrm.name]?

\n\n“It’s the natural way of our kind, of course,” she says matter-of-factly, proud of her learned knowledge of her kind. “We remain with our Qim and Qal to have our every need met until you two are summoned by the Grand Qim. That includes food, shelter, and any needs regarding mating

\n\nYou tell her that you and [frostwyrm.name] aren’t exactly of the same race. “Well, I gathered that much, silly,” she laughs, nuzzling your nose with hers. “I think it’s incredible that the Grand Qim managed to put you two together, despite your differences.”

\n\nWhat you mean is that, where you come from, there is no such thing as ‘inner circle coupling,’ and that children – or, kips – from your kind do not mate with their parents, and leave the home once they reach a certain age.

\n\n“Why would they want to do any of that?” she asks. You reply that there’s a specific word for ‘inner circle coupling,’ and it’s considered a taboo act. Not everyone appreciates the idea of mating with their parents. “Why wouldn’t they? My Qim is the biggest of her kind, and my Qal is the smartest and the strongest. I can’t imagine mating with anyone else. Isn’t that the same with everyone?”

\n\nYou appreciate the compliment, but no, not everyone thinks the same way as the Frostwyrms. Everyone has a different approach to mating, but some laws and concerns are universal: society, which is another word to describe the way a civilization thinks and acts, looks down on ‘inner circle coupling’ as a serious crime.

\n\n“Is it society that’s wrong, then? Who does it hurt to mate with who you want?” You tell her that that’s a very dangerous line of thinking – she can’t just ‘mate with who she wants.’ Her partner needs to be aware and willing to mate with her, and if they aren’t, that’s an even more serious crime. “It sounds to me like your ‘society’ is a boring, hard place to be. There are a lot of rules to keep in mind.” You don’t budge, and you force your kip to promise that she won’t mate with anyone or anything without their consent. “Okay, fine! I promise.”
\n\nWith that squared away, you relax.

\n\n“Does this mean you aren’t okay with us celebrating our inner circle coupling with our Qim?” she asks, her eyes wide with concern. “I mean, you’re not from our Qim’s kind, and your kind doesn’t like the idea of a parent mating with their child.”

\n\nYou tell her that you’ve come to accept that it’s a part of Frostwyrm culture, and you couldn’t ask [frostwyrm.name] to be something she’s not, in her own home, for your sake. You don’t mind if she satiates the sexual needs of your kips, and it’s enough for you to know that she and your kips all respect your decision as their Qal to not participate.
\n\n“Okay,” she says, snuggling in closer to you and burying her nose into the crease of your neck affectionately. “I hope I’m as understanding as you are with my own mate, my Qal.”

\n\nAnd, that said, it’s not like [pc.originalRace] societies and cultures are all that different from Frostwyrm ideals. For example, your kind enjoys the concept of mating for life. Although, they don’t call it ‘mating,’ even though that’s what you do; they call it ‘marriage.’

\n\n“What’s the difference?” she asks, and you tell her that there isn’t really one; it’s just a fancy label that your kind apply to mates to tell others that they aren’t available. “Why would you need a label for that? Shouldn’t their scent markings be enough?” You smile. That’s a kinky thought, but most people try to be as un-smelly as possible.
\n\n“So marriage just means that you don’t mate with anyone except your mate?” That’s about the short of it, you confirm.

“Does that mean we’re married to our Qim?” Well – suddenly, you’ve gotten yourself in a bit of a situation. You want to tell her that, if she’s married to anyone in the lair, it would be you, but you just said that two married people only mate with their mates. So... in a sense, yes, you suppose, [frostwyrm.name] is married to your kip
. To all your kips
. You feel like you’re suddenly starring in a sitcom.

\n\nUsing her psionics, "+(void 0!==flags.NYKKE_MET&&e?"Nykke":"your kip")+" reaches out to the rest of the clutch, exclaiming that, as far as their Qal’s society is concerned, they’re all married to [frostwyrm.name]! The exclamation is met almost entirely with confusion, but when she explains that it’s a good thing, they all cheer in delight. This is her life now, you guess.

You’re cuddled warmly against [frostwyrm.name] and your eldest daughter resting up against you, both of them sharing your body heat, as you stare out to the village of Frostwyrmlings you and [frostwyrm.name] have created, and towards the offshoot cave that Nykke seems to perpetually work on. It’s been many years since your firstborn, and not only do you feel as young as ever, and not only do you not intend to stop breeding with your Qim, but you feel as though your adventure as the Qal to your race of hybrids is only beginning.

\n\nYour two Qims love you with all their heart; you don’t need psionics to know that. And your kips are all highly devoted to you; their love toward their Qim and their Qal isn’t something you’d ever question. Your family in the icy lair would do absolutely anything for you, if you’d but ask.

\n\n[frostwyrm.name] is an enormous, icy dragon, capable of rending buildings and small armies asunder with her bulk. Nykke and each of your daughters are highly muscled; tall as a giant; and naturally equipped with scales as tough as shields. If you asked them to, and you were serious about it, you could easily take over Uveto; the only defense the planetoid has is its indigenous peoples and weather stations full of scientists, not fighters. It’d be a fairly simple, and possibly bloodless, operation.

\n\nYou look out to the village of your daughters, each of them milling about the lair with their own thoughts, concerns, and activities, waiting for you to simply say a word, any word, and they’ll drop everything for you. It’s very tempting. But it would also mean abandoning your by-now years-long adventure to claim your inheritance from your late father and being a full-time Qal to the Frostwyrms.
\n\nDo you stop your adventuring and claim Uveto as your kingdom?

textify(Ul||(Ul=(0,C.Z)(["
		It might just be age creeping up on you, but you feel a sense of belonging that you can’t rightly explain, here in the lair. You’ve been with [frostwyrm.name] for so many years and you’ve had so many kips that you can’t shake the feeling that this is... where you belong. Here, as the Qal to your new, invigorated race of Frostwyrm ","hybrids; where you should stay until you, [frostwyrm.name][frostwyrm.andNykke|, Nykke,] and [flurry.name] are all summoned by the Grand Qim and your daughters"," leave to spread their genes, just as you have, throughout all of Uveto. Possibly further.

Something that tells the recipient that they’re on the giver’s mind and that they’ve put some thought into their wants and needs.
\n\n
Immediately, though, you’re at a loss as to what you give to [frostwyrm.name] – she’s a Frostwyrm. You can think of a few wants and needs that she has, but
you’ve already given her what she wants. Sure, you two could ‘make’ some more, and you’ll have a ton of fun doing it, but you think ‘more kips’ would be a lazy, uninitiated gift to give her
you’re working on one of them right now, and it’s not something that’s going to be ‘done’ in time for the holidays
. What sort of gifts would Frostwyrms appreciate, and can you even make one from this island? Food, baths, and sex are the obvious choices....

\n\nWhile you mull the decision over, you fill [frostwyrm.name] on the tradition of gift-giving between individuals during these times, and you explain what usually goes into the thought process of getting a gift for another. This is a simple matter, my mate, she says unsubtly. The greatest gift you could give your Qim is additional kips to further our lineage. You answer that, yes, that was one of the first thoughts you had, but procreation is something that you two do regardless of the time of year. This is an opportunity for you both to show your appreciation in a way you otherwise wouldn’t.

\n\n[frostwyrm.name] bends her head downward contemplatively. Perhaps you are right. Every moment together with you is itself a gift, my Qal, yet I fear that our moments would lose their luster as they become routine. Her four red eyes lock onto yours attentively. What would you desire of your Qim in this occasion, my Qal?

\n\nTelling her what you want would ruin the surprise, though – and the message. The point of the gift is that you’re supposed to be paying enough attention to the recipient that they don’t need to tell you what they want, and that you should just know. For that reason, you aren’t going to ask her what she wants, either.
\n\nHer breath comes out as a low, thoughtful growl. This may prove difficult, she says. That’s the spirit!

\n\nYou ask [frostwyrm.name] if she wouldn’t mind giving you a lift to Irestead, along the coast where you two usually meet. It’s a long flight, but the time will give you both the opportunity to think on what it is you can get each other. She agrees, and in a moment, you and [frostwyrm.name] take to the sky
, your daughter flying alongside you both
, your daughters taking their place on either side of their Qim in the air
, flying in a tight V behind her
, on your way to Irestead.

\n\nYou promised to call out to her within, uh... well before Light’s Fall, whether or not your trip is successful. When you hit the mainland, you go your separate ways. The flight lasted an hour, and you still don’t have the first idea what to get her. But, you’ll have an easier time thinking about it when you’re in your own element, or so you tell yourself.

\n\nThere aren’t a lot of material goods or stores to buy things from in Irestead. It’s a weather station that doubles as a landing point for interplanetary visitors; there are weather-measuring instruments and lots of alcohol, but nothing a Frostwyrm would be interested in. Although, to be fair to yourself, you’re sure [frostwyrm.name]’s never had any alcohol – you don’t know if she wouldn’t like it. And you’re sure an inebriated Frostwyrm would be a sight to see.

\n\nThat said, Irestead is much more festive than you had expected, coming into it. The local scientists and explorers are taking whatever time they can off their busy schedules to celebrate their own holidays, including putting up decorations. You even catch a whiff of some baked goods coming from their mess hall. It’s as good a place to start, you figure.

\n\nYou’re greeted with the sight of enough baked cookies and cakes to give everyone on the station at least one of each. You ask the baker, a stout and jolly man, if he could spare enough to feed a Frostwyrm"+(void 0!==flags.FROSTWYRM_KIP_COUNT?" and its family":"")+", you were given a fairly curt ‘no’ – the dough is used in too many different pastries to possibly spare that many. But, you’re instead given three large packs of frosting: red, green, and white. There aren’t nearly enough uses for them and they’re going to go bad well before the holidays are over, so you’re welcome to them. It’s, uh, a start, you suppose.

\n\nYou wander Irestead aimlessly, wracking your mind for something that [frostwyrm.name] would enjoy, and you doubt three big packs of food flavoring is really going to cut it. You glance out the windows to the frozen expanse of Uveto and try to think of something she, as a Frostwyrm, might appreciate, but every time you think of something, you just remind yourself that there’s nothing out there that she can’t just get herself.

\n\nAs time grows short, you start exploring the more practical shops of Uveto, and you happen to come across a thermal jacket, on the rack for a modest price. It’s lined with fur and it doesn’t look thick enough to provide a lot of protection in the coldest of colds, but it would nonetheless provide something. [frostwyrm.name] doesn’t have any fur at all and she’s out there, bearing Uveto’s climate with just her scales. Maybe she’d appreciate something a little warm?

\n\nThe jacket was on a holiday sale"+(pc.credits<264?", thank the Gods":"")+", and you have your gift to [frostwyrm.name]. Naturally, the whole thing isn’t going to fit a Frostwyrm, so you make some modifications – namely, once you’re out of the store and somewhere relatively private, you rip off the sleeves. Now, your gift is ready.

\n\nThe trek back to the Uveto coast is long and cold, but you’re fuelled by your excitement at meeting [frostwyrm.name] again and giving her the gift you’ve picked out. You call to her and say that you’re ready to return. At once, my Qal, she replies, and in another moment, she’s by your side and you’re on your way back.

\n\nYou notice that her scales appear slightly different – a little bluer in shade, across most of her body. Normally, she’s rather resilient to the cold, but it appears now that her body’s been slightly frosted over.

\n\nWere you successful in your search, my mate? [frostwyrm.name] asks you as she flies back. You tell her that you... think you did, but you can’t be certain that she’ll appreciate it. As you taught me, it is not the gift that is essential. It is the intention behind it. I trust that my Qal has only the best of intentions in mind.
\n\nThe flight back is chilly and long
, made warmer by your clutch keeping you company the entire way

. The entire flight, you feel what seems to be apprehension from [frostwyrm.name]; if you had to guess, she’s just as concerned and nervous that you’ll enjoy whatever gift she had picked out for you. The suspense is usually one of the harder parts of the holidays.

\n\nAfter another hour, [frostwyrm.name]’s lair appears on the horizon, and you make a graceful landing back onto the snow of the island. Is this when we present each other our gifts? [frostwyrm.name] asks you, her body shuffling to block you from the entrance of the lair.

Your "+(flags.FROSTWYRM_KIP_COUNT>1?"daughters move":"daughter moves")+" with her, attempting to nonchalantly prevent your eyes from peeping at something in the lair’s direction. They aren’t being especially subtle about it, but you play along for now.

\n\nYou respond affirmatively, and you reach for your gift to her: a torn thermal jacket, and its two sleeves. [frostwyrm.name] studies them curiously: flimsy fabrics that would barely fit around her leg, much less her entire body, but you respond that that’s the idea. Unfortunately, Irestead had lots to offer for kinds and individuals like yours, but not a lot to provide for Frostwyrms, so you had to get a little creative.

\n\nYou ask [frostwyrm.name] to bend her neck down so that you can get to her horns, and she does so. You explain that you can’t really survive in the cold the same way she can, and that you need help to keep warm in the colder climates. Then, you thought, your Qim probably didn’t enjoy the cold all that much, so maybe – just maybe – she would appreciate trying to keep warm. To that end, you slip the sleeves over her horns, and once those are in place, you wrap the body of the jacket around her foremost left leg and zip it up, giving her a warm, fluffy cufflink.

\n\n[frostwyrm.name] regards the jacket zipped up against her, studying it with two red eyes, then turning her head to see it with the other two. You realize that it isn’t much, and it’s not going to keep most of her warm.

\n\n[pc.name], she begins, placing her foot in the snow and watching the jacket protect her from the snow, I... I had never considered that there could be methods to prevent the elements from coating my form. Every journey into the Evercold was a tasking, unforgiving event that no one individual of my kind ever enjoyed. She steps forward, her long tongue snaking out and giving you a warm, loving lick across most of your body. I can only hope my gift to you is half as considerate, my mate and my Qal.

\n\n[frostwyrm.name] looks over her flank, communicating with your daughters to retrieve the gift they had made for their Qal. Eager, they disappear into the lair, and only a moment later, they return with their gift to you: a set of scales, woven to link together, the suit a slightly darker shade of blue than [frostwyrm.name]’s body. It’s about tailored to the size of your torso.

\n\nWe, as your clutch, understand that you have responsibilities to your culture that do not include us. There are times when your kind must summon you away from us, and to the dangers of the world around us. Your oldest daughter lays the small sheet of Frostwyrm scales along both her hands, presenting it to you as a gift. The sheet consists of two layers: the top layer is covered with smaller scales, interlinked with each other inseparably, and the lower layer includes a single, massive scale underneath. Your clutch and I have woven this small protective layer for you, our Qal.
Though your body has its own protection, it is not of the same temperament as my kind.
As your own body is unprotected and exposed, this will provide you with the protection of my kind.
Our intent is to protect our Qal, no matter the distance – and to remind you of your clutch, wherever you go.

\n\n[frostwyrm.name] turns on the spot, her tail digging through the snow until she feels it clank against something she had hidden in it. Using her claws, she unearths it and presents it to you: one of her scales, a slightly deeper shade of blue than the rest of her. Her one scale is about the size of your torso, and it’s hard as rocks, if not harder.

\n\nI understand that, as you are not of my kind, you have other responsibilities and demands that your culture makes of you. You cannot spend all your time with your clutch, as much as I desire it so. She nudges her scale to you, pressing it into your hands. This scale of mine has been tempered in the cold, heavy waters beneath the ocean’s surface. It is stronger and more resilient than my natural form.
You have fine scales of your own, my Qal, but they do not have the same temperament or provide the same defense as my own.
You do not possess scales of your own, my Qal; your body is frail and exposed to the world.
My intent is to give you a part of myself so that you may protect yourself from the dangers of the world.

\n\nYou humbly take the "+(flags.FROSTWYRM_KIP_COUNT>=10?"suit of scales":"scale")+" into your arms. It’s lightweight; totally opaque; and you can already tell it’s sturdy as could possibly be. You run your hand along the surface, "+(flags.FROSTWYRM_KIP_COUNT>=10?"your fingers tickling along all the interlocking scales, making them each clink against each other":"running your fingers along the smoothness of [frostwyrm.name]’s scale")+". Your "+(flags.FROSTWYRM_KIP_COUNT>=10?"clutch":"Qim")+" had done this... for you. To make sure you’d always have something to remind you of "+(flags.FROSTWYRM_KIP_COUNT>=10?"them":"her")+".

\n\nYou tell [frostwyrm.name] that it’s easily one of the most thoughtful gifts you had ever received. She’s right: it’s dangerous out there, adventuring in the world. There are a lot of things that could hurt you, and sometimes, you can get a little homesick when you’re away from the lair for too long. With this, you’ll have the power of the Frostwyrms on your side. You won’t have any reason to fear anything with this as your armor.
\n\nIt is a privilege to hear you say those words, my Qal, [frostwyrm.name] says, craning her long neck down to nuzzle against you.
\n\nWhat other activities does your kind’s tradition include, my Qal? [frostwyrm.name] asks.
\n\n
